title: About
text:
Stat et al. is a cumulative statistics Q&A series — statistics, and its co-authors. Each numbered entry asks one question, answers it from first principles, and builds on everything that came before.
The series is written by a marketing PhD student relearning statistics deliberately, from foundations through graduate level, and is aimed at readers doing the same: PhD students in marketing and the social sciences who want to understand why the methods work, not just how to run them.

title: Stat et al.
text:

Q1: What is a data set?



beginner



A data set is a collection of observations.





Jul 10, 2026





No matching items

title: Q1: What is a data set?
text: A data set is a collection of observations. Here is an example of a data set that contains names, college majors, and final exam scores of five students in a statistics course - | Observation | Name | Major | Score | |—————–|———-|————|———–| | 1 | Andrew | Statistics | 91 | | 2 | Becky | Marketing | 85 | | 3 | Charlie | Psychology | 87 | | 4 | Cynthia | Statistics | 89 | | 5 | Dexter | Marketing | 92 |

title: Glossary
text:
Terms are added here the first time they appear in the series, with a link back to the question that introduced them. Alphabetical within each letter group.

The glossary grows with the series. First entries arrive with Q1.
